classClosure.reason: One-shot, per-call child-process reap: when a single codex exec-json call's turn completes but its OWN child lingers past the grace, that one child is reaped once (SIGTERM + a single unref'd SIGKILL backstop) at that call's settlement. No loop, respawn, retry, or feedback controller — the reap count equals the call count, itself bounded by the host spawn-cap. Not a self-triggered control loop.
